title: CI: new graph-based pod rm -fa flake
body:
New flake, most likely caused by https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25169, so I guess it is fair to say the locking is not sound... 

```
           # podman-remote [options] pod rm -fa -t 0

           fatal error: concurrent map writes
           fatal error: concurrent map writes
         
           goroutine 358 [running, locked to thread]:
           github.com/containers/podman/v5/libpod.removeContainerGraph.func1(0xc000791ea0, 0xc00065bc50)
```

https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4575829654503424/html/int-remote-debian-13-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-kube-play-test-volumes-from-annotation-with-source-container-in-pod--1

comments.body:
Ack, I’ll take a look today

On Tue, Feb 11, 2025 at 05:50 Paul Holzinger ***@***.***>
wrote:

> Assigned #25289 <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25289> to
> @mheon <https://github.com/mheon>.
>
> —
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25289#event-16262564816>, or
> unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCC72QNHRHCE5EIGJQD2PHIXZAVCNFSM6AAAAABW4XDS4GVHI2DSMVQWIX3LMV45UABCJFZXG5LFIV3GK3TUJZXXI2LGNFRWC5DJN5XDWMJWGI3DENJWGQ4DCNQ>
> .
> You are receiving this because you were assigned.Message ID:
> ***@***.***>
>

Cause identified.
I think I'm going to whip up a generic concurrent-map type (like `sync.Map` except it doesn't have to use `interface{}`) in a util package and replace the maps in that function with it. Would make things cleaner, I think.
